title: Register recovery agent contract
description: Introduce the `recovery` agent type across the agent contract surface, including schema/template registration and minimal runtime behavior needed for a no-tool recovery agent.
acceptance_criteria: `recovery` is accepted anywhere agent types are schema-validated for agent output.
`AGENT_SKILL_ALLOWLIST` grants recovery agents only the minimal orchestrator skill set.
Runner behavior supports recovery agents in no-tool or minimal-tool mode.
`templates/agents/recovery.md` exists and instructs the agent to emit only the required JSON object.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `recovery` to AGENT_TYPES and BEAD_TYPES in models.py. Added recovery to AGENT_SKILL_ALLOWLIST with only `core/base-orchestrator`. Added `recovery` to both AGENT_OUTPUT_SCHEMA and PLANNER_OUTPUT_SCHEMA new_beads/plan_child agent_type enums (in runner.py and prompts.py). Added no-tool override in ClaudeCodeAgentRunner._exec_json_with_response for recovery agent type (tools=[]) so claude gets --allowedTools "". Added recovery to BUILT_IN_AGENT_TYPES in prompts.py. Added recovery to agent_types in OrchestratorConfig default and default_config() in config.py. Created templates/agents/recovery.md with guardrails instructing the agent to emit only JSON with no tool calls.
handoff_summary.remaining: N/A — all acceptance criteria satisfied. Scheduler integration (auto-creating recovery beads on no-structured-output blocks and handling recovery completion) is a separate bead per the spec.
handoff_summary.risks: The `config.py` change is outside the declared expected_files scope but is necessary for `supported_agent_types()` validation to pass when config is loaded — without it, `load_guardrail_template("recovery", ...)` would raise ValueError. The prompts.py AGENT_OUTPUT_SCHEMA was already partially present (build_recovery_prompt added by a prior run); the enum update is additive and consistent.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed to tester/review beads. Tester should verify: (1) AGENT_TYPES includes recovery, (2) schema validation accepts recovery in new_beads, (3) allowed_skill_ids("recovery") returns only core/base-orchestrator, (4) ClaudeCodeAgentRunner resolves empty tools for recovery agent type, (5) load_guardrail_template("recovery") finds the template, (6) existing beads without recovery fields still load correctly.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: prompts.py and runner.py are touched files; any downstream bead that also modifies these files should be aware of the new `recovery` entries in enum lists and the no-tool guard in _exec_json_with_response.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: No-tool mode for recovery is implemented as a hardcoded guard in ClaudeCodeAgentRunner._exec_json_with_response (`if agent_type == "recovery": tools = []`). This is preferred over a config-based mechanism (e.g. `no_tools_agents` set in BackendConfig) because: (a) recovery is the only agent type requiring this behavior per the spec, (b) the config change would add surface area outside this bead's scope, and (c) the behavior must be enforced regardless of config overrides. The `core/base-orchestrator` skill is the only skill granted to recovery agents — no role, capability, or task skills — matching the spec's intent that recovery agents do no real work.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: AGENT_TYPES and BEAD_TYPES contain 'recovery'; allowed_skill_ids('recovery') == ['core/base-orchestrator']; AGENT_OUTPUT_SCHEMA and PLANNER_OUTPUT_SCHEMA new_beads/plan_child enums include 'recovery'; ClaudeCodeAgentRunner resolves empty tools list for recovery agent_type; load_guardrail_template('recovery', root=Path('.')) finds templates/agents/recovery.md; supported_agent_types() includes recovery; existing bead JSON without recovery_for still deserializes correctly.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The CodexAgentRunner does not have an equivalent no-tool override for recovery (no `--allowedTools` flag in codex CLI). Recovery beads should only be dispatched via the Claude backend. Scheduler integration (auto-creating recovery beads and completing originals) is not part of this bead.

conflict_risks: prompts.py and runner.py are touched files; any downstream bead that also modifies these files should be aware of the new `recovery` entries in enum lists and the no-tool guard in _exec_json_with_response.

execution_history.summary: Registered the `recovery` agent type across the full agent contract surface: schema enums, skill allowlist, runner no-tool mode, guardrail template, and config defaults.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
